sanskrit: सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श्रृणु मे परमं वचः । इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ॥
hindiTranslation: सबसे अत्यन्त गोपनीय वचन तू फिर मेरेसे सुन ।  तू मेरा अत्यन्त प्रिय है, इसलिये मैं तेरे हितकी बात कहूँगा ॥
englishTranslation: Listen again to My most secret word. You are dear to Me, so I will speak for your good.
essence: The deepest teaching is spoken as care, not command.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to hear one more secret teaching. Because Arjuna is dear to him, Krishna will speak only what is truly beneficial.
simpleInsight: Being loved means being told what helps, not just what comforts.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra field, Arjuna has already been told the deepest teaching: surrender to Krishna. Krishna now pauses before the final command and says there is still a more secret word to hear. He speaks as a teacher who cares personally for Arjuna's welfare.
modernRelevance: A friend stops you before you make the wrong call and says, 'Hear me out once more — I’m telling you this because I care.' That moment can change everything.
translationLiteral: सर्वगुह्यतमं (most secret) / भूयः (again) / श्रृणु (listen) / मे (my) / परमं (highest) / वचः (word) / इष्टः असि (you are dear) / मे (to me) / दृढम् (truly) / इति (thus) / ततः (therefore) / वक्ष्यामि (I will speak) / ते (to you) / हितम् (what is good)
insight: It shifts the listener from passive hearing to trusting that the next instruction is meant for their good.
